etymology.description: semantic 广(廣) (guǎng, "shelter") + phonetic 黃(黃) (huáng, "yellow") = area underneath a shelter -> broad
etymology.interpretation: 廣 combines the semantic element of shelter or building represented by 广(廣) with the phonetic element of 黃(黃), suggesting an area or space that is open and extensive, consistent with the idea of something being 'broad' or 'wide'.
etymology.simplification: 廣 was simplified by reducing the component 黃(黃) to the simpler form 广(廣), retaining the semantic aspect while simplifying the structure for phonetic indication.

examples.chinese: 这条路很广(這條路很廣)。
广场上有许多人(廣場上有許多人)。
examples.english: There are many people in the square.

examples.chinese: 她的知识面很广(她的知識面很廣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de zhīshì miàn hěn guǎng.
examples.english: She has a broad range of knowledge.
